SOURCE: 01_employee_handbook.pdf | págs. 1-2
TITLE: Política de Licencias: Tipos y Requisitos

1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Employee Handbook

 HR Knowledge Base — Version 3.1 | Effective January 2025

 1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Study / Exam Leave 5 days/year 4 weeks minimum

Volunteering 2 days/year 2 weeks minimum

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following
 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

[PÁGINA 2]
Violations are investigated under the Disciplinary Policy and may result in termination. Anonymous
reports can be submitted via the Ethics Hotline.

5. Benefits

Health & Wellness
 • Private health insurance (employee + 1 dependent included)
 • Mental health support: 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health
 • Gym allowance: €50/month reimbursed

Financial
 • Pension contribution: 5% employer match
 • Annual performance bonus: 5–20% of base salary
 • Employee stock options (ESOP): Level Senior and above

Learning & Development
 • €1,500/year L&D; budget per employee
 • Access to Linked In Learning, O'Reilly and Coursera
 • Internal mentoring programme — apply via HR portal

 Questions? Contact the HR team at hr@hrkb.internal | HR Portal: intranet/hr

SOURCE: 04_compensation_and_benefits.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Resumen de Beneficios para Empleados

[PÁGINA 2]
--- DATOS TABULARES (Pág 2) ---
| Benefit | Detail |
| --- | --- |
| Health insurance | Full private cover, employee + 1 dependent |
| Pension | 5% employer match (up to 5% employee contribution) |
| Gym allowance | €50/month, reimbursed via expenses |
| Home office setup | €500 one-time + €30/month stipend |
| L&D budget | €1,500/year per employee |
| Mental health | 8 therapy sessions/year (Oliva Health) |
| Meal allowance | €8/working day (card provided) |
| Transport | €60/month public transport card |
| Referral bonus | €1,000 per successful hire (paid at 3 months) |
-----------------------------

Employees at Senior level and above receive stock options upon joining and at each promotion.
Vesting schedule: 4 years with 1-year cliff.

 • 25% vests after year 1 (cliff)
 • Remaining 75% vests monthly over years 2–4
 • Exercise window: 90 days after leaving the company

Benefits Summary

Benefit Detail
per_question.ground_truth: Employees are entitled to 25 days of annual leave per year.

Key Systems & Tools

System Purpose Access

Slack Internal communication IT Day 1

Notion Documentation & wikis IT Day 1

JIRA Project & task tracking Manager request

Bamboo HR HR portal (leave, payslips) HR Day 1

Google Workspace Email, Docs, Calendar IT Day 1

 Lattice Performance & OKRs HR Week 1

Probation Period
The probation period lasts 90 days. During this time you will have structured check-ins with your
manager at day 30, 60, and 90. At the end of probation, your manager will confirm your employment
in writing. Remote working and stock options are not available during probation.

Who to Contact

Need Contact

IT issues it-support@hrkb.internal | Slack: #it-help

HR questions hr@hrkb.internal | Slack: #hr-team

 Payroll & expenses finance@hrkb.internal

Mental health support wellbeing@hrkb.internal (confidential)

Ethics concerns ethics-hotline@hrkb.internal (anonymous)

 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

[PÁGINA 2]
Violations are investigated under the Disciplinary Policy and may result in termination. Anonymous
reports can be submitted via the Ethics Hotline.

5. Benefits

Health & Wellness
 • Private health insurance (employee + 1 dependent included)
 • Mental health support: 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health
 • Gym allowance: €50/month reimbursed

Financial
 • Pension contribution: 5% employer match
 • Annual performance bonus: 5–20% of base salary
 • Employee stock options (ESOP): Level Senior and above

Learning & Development
 • €1,500/year L&D; budget per employee
 • Access to Linked In Learning, O'Reilly and Coursera
 • Internal mentoring programme — apply via HR portal

 Questions? Contact the HR team at hr@hrkb.internal | HR Portal: intranet/hr
per_question.ground_truth: The company provides 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health as part of the mental health support benefit.

per_question.question: What is the parental leave entitlement for primary and secondary caregivers?
per_question.answer: The parental leave entitlement is 16 weeks for primary caregivers and 6 weeks for secondary caregivers, with an 8 weeks minimum requirement.

per_question.answer: The self-assessment phase opens from 1–15 November.

-----------------------------

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

SOURCE: 03_onboarding_guide.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Programa de Onboarding para Nuevos Empleados

Day 1 Checklist

Time Activity Owner

09:00 Welcome meeting with HR HR

10:00 IT setup & security training IT

11:00 Meet your manager and team Manager

12:30 Team lunch Team

14:00 Company values & culture session HR

15:30 Benefits enrolment walkthrough HR

16:30 Wrap-up & Q&A Manager

30 / 60 / 90 Day Goals

First 30 Days — Learn
 • Complete all mandatory training (GDPR, Security, Anti-bribery)
 • Shadow at least 3 different team members
 • Set up 1:1s with key stakeholders
 • Understand your team's OKRs for the current quarter

Days 31–60 — Contribute
 • Take ownership of at least one small project or task
 • Present a 'learnings so far' to your manager
 • Join at least one cross-functional meeting

Days 61–90 — Deliver
 • Complete your first formal goal-setting with manager
 • Deliver your first independent piece of work
 • Complete the 90-day survey (sent automatically by HR)
per_question.ground_truth: The self-assessment phase opens on 1 November and closes on 15 November. Manager reviews follow from 16 to 30 November.

per_question.question: What score is required to be eligible for a promotion?
per_question.answer: A score of 4.0 or above in the last review is required to be eligible for a promotion.

TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)

 • Deliver your first independent piece of work

SOURCE: 01_employee_handbook.pdf | págs. 1-2
TITLE: Política de Licencias: Tipos y Requisitos

1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

SOURCE: 01_employee_handbook.pdf | págs. 1-2
TITLE: Política de Licencias: Tipos y Requisitos

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Study / Exam Leave 5 days/year 4 weeks minimum

Volunteering 2 days/year 2 weeks minimum

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following
 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Condiciones para Ofertas de Empleo y Verificación

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

in writing. Remote working and stock options are not available during probation.

-----------------------------

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

per_question.question: What is the salary band for a Senior level employee?
per_question.answer: The salary band for a Senior level employee is 55,000 – 80,000 EUR per year.

TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

Compensation & Benefits Policy

Effective January 2025 | Confidential

Salary Bands
Salaries are structured around 5 levels applicable across all departments. Bands are reviewed
annually against market benchmarks (Radford / Mercer surveys). We target the 65th percentile of
the market for base salary.

Level Role Examples Band (EUR / year)

Junior Associate, Analyst I 28,000 – 38,000

Mid Specialist, Analyst II 38,000 – 55,000

Senior Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst 55,000 – 80,000

Lead Tech Lead, Team Lead 75,000 – 100,000

Manager Manager, Head of 85,000 – 120,000

Annual Pay Review
Pay reviews take place in January each year, effective 1 February. Increases are based on:
individual performance score, market movement, and position within band.

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Salary Bands

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as

Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

contexts:
SOURCE: 04_compensation_and_benefits.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

SOURCE: 02_performance_review_guide.pdf | págs. 1-2
TITLE: Guía de Evaluación del Desempeño 2025

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

SOURCE: 02_performance_review_guide.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Guía de Evaluación del Desempeño 2025

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

Employee Handbook

 HR Knowledge Base — Version 3.1 | Effective January 2025

 1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Study / Exam Leave 5 days/year 4 weeks minimum

Volunteering 2 days/year 2 weeks minimum

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following
 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

[PÁGINA 2]
Violations are investigated under the Disciplinary Policy and may result in termination. Anonymous
reports can be submitted via the Ethics Hotline.

5. Benefits

Health & Wellness
 • Private health insurance (employee + 1 dependent included)
 • Mental health support: 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health
 • Gym allowance: €50/month reimbursed

Financial
 • Pension contribution: 5% employer match
 • Annual performance bonus: 5–20% of base salary
 • Employee stock options (ESOP): Level Senior and above

Learning & Development
 • €1,500/year L&D; budget per employee
 • Access to Linked In Learning, O'Reilly and Coursera
 • Internal mentoring programme — apply via HR portal

 Questions? Contact the HR team at hr@hrkb.internal | HR Portal: intranet/hr

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

-----------------------------

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Condiciones para Ofertas de Empleo y Verificación

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Diversity & Inclusion

 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

per_question.question: What topics are prohibited in job interviews?
per_question.answer:
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

- Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
- Marital status or family plans
- Nationality or country of origin
- Religious beliefs or political views
- Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Condiciones para Ofertas de Empleo y Verificación

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

SOURCE: 04_compensation_and_benefits.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

TITLE: Condiciones para Ofertas de Empleo y Verificación

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.
per_question.ground_truth: Primary caregivers receive 16 weeks of parental leave. Secondary caregivers receive 6 weeks. Notice required is 8 weeks minimum.

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

TITLE: Proceso de Reclutamiento y Selección

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Proceso de Reclutamiento y Selección

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Proceso de Reclutamiento y Selección

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

Probation Period
The probation period lasts 90 days. During this time you will have structured check-ins with your

in writing. Remote working and stock options are not available during probation.

1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

 1. Welcome

 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following

Financial

Employee Handbook

 HR Knowledge Base — Version 3.1 | Effective January 2025

 1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Study / Exam Leave 5 days/year 4 weeks minimum

Volunteering 2 days/year 2 weeks minimum

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following
 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

[PÁGINA 2]
Violations are investigated under the Disciplinary Policy and may result in termination. Anonymous
reports can be submitted via the Ethics Hotline.

5. Benefits

Health & Wellness
 • Private health insurance (employee + 1 dependent included)
 • Mental health support: 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health
 • Gym allowance: €50/month reimbursed

Financial
 • Pension contribution: 5% employer match
 • Annual performance bonus: 5–20% of base salary
 • Employee stock options (ESOP): Level Senior and above

Learning & Development
 • €1,500/year L&D; budget per employee
 • Access to Linked In Learning, O'Reilly and Coursera
 • Internal mentoring programme — apply via HR portal

 Questions? Contact the HR team at hr@hrkb.internal | HR Portal: intranet/hr

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 1
TITLE: Proceso de Reclutamiento y Selección

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices
per_question.ground_truth: In the first 30 days employees should complete all mandatory training (GDPR, Security, Anti-bribery), shadow at least 3 team members, set up 1:1s with key stakeholders, and understand the team's OKRs for the current quarter.

-----------------------------

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

per_question.question: What is the salary band for a Senior level employee?
per_question.answer: The salary band for a Senior level employee is 55,000 – 80,000 EUR per year.

TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

[PÁGINA 1]
--- DATOS TABULARES (Pág 1) ---
| Level | Role Examples | Band (EUR / year) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Junior | Associate, Analyst I | 28,000 – 38,000 |
| Mid | Specialist, Analyst II | 38,000 – 55,000 |
| Senior | Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst | 55,000 – 80,000 |
| Lead | Tech Lead, Team Lead | 75,000 – 100,000 |
| Manager | Manager, Head of | 85,000 – 120,000 |

| Performance Score | Typical Increase |
| --- | --- |
| Outstanding (4.5–5.0) | 6 – 10% |
| Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) | 3 – 6% |
| Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) | 1 – 3% |
| Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) | 0% (PIP in place) |
-----------------------------

Compensation & Benefits Policy

Effective January 2025 | Confidential

Salary Bands
Salaries are structured around 5 levels applicable across all departments. Bands are reviewed
annually against market benchmarks (Radford / Mercer surveys). We target the 65th percentile of
the market for base salary.

Level Role Examples Band (EUR / year)

Junior Associate, Analyst I 28,000 – 38,000

Mid Specialist, Analyst II 38,000 – 55,000

Senior Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst 55,000 – 80,000

Lead Tech Lead, Team Lead 75,000 – 100,000

Manager Manager, Head of 85,000 – 120,000

Annual Pay Review
Pay reviews take place in January each year, effective 1 February. Increases are based on:
individual performance score, market movement, and position within band.

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

per_question.question: How does the stock option vesting schedule work?
per_question.answer: Employees at Senior level and above receive stock options with a vesting schedule of 4 years and a 1-year cliff. 25% vests after the first year (cliff), and the remaining 75% vests monthly over years 2 to 4. There is a 90-day exercise window after leaving the company.

-----------------------------

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

Compensation & Benefits Policy

Effective January 2025 | Confidential

Salary Bands
Salaries are structured around 5 levels applicable across all departments. Bands are reviewed
annually against market benchmarks (Radford / Mercer surveys). We target the 65th percentile of
the market for base salary.

Level Role Examples Band (EUR / year)

Junior Associate, Analyst I 28,000 – 38,000

Mid Specialist, Analyst II 38,000 – 55,000

Senior Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst 55,000 – 80,000

Lead Tech Lead, Team Lead 75,000 – 100,000

Manager Manager, Head of 85,000 – 120,000

Annual Pay Review
Pay reviews take place in January each year, effective 1 February. Increases are based on:
individual performance score, market movement, and position within band.

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

Compensation & Benefits Policy

Effective January 2025 | Confidential

Salary Bands
Salaries are structured around 5 levels applicable across all departments. Bands are reviewed
annually against market benchmarks (Radford / Mercer surveys). We target the 65th percentile of
the market for base salary.

Level Role Examples Band (EUR / year)

Junior Associate, Analyst I 28,000 – 38,000

Mid Specialist, Analyst II 38,000 – 55,000

Senior Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst 55,000 – 80,000

Lead Tech Lead, Team Lead 75,000 – 100,000

Manager Manager, Head of 85,000 – 120,000

Annual Pay Review
Pay reviews take place in January each year, effective 1 February. Increases are based on:
individual performance score, market movement, and position within band.

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

Needs Improvement (1.0–2.0) 0% (PIP in place)

Bonus Structure
Annual performance bonuses are paid in March, based on the prior year's results. Target bonus as
a % of base salary:

 • Junior / Mid: 5%
 • Senior: 10%
 • Lead: 15%
 • Manager: 20%
Actual payout ranges from 0% to 2x target depending on individual and company performance.
Employees must be employed on 31 December to be eligible.

Stock Options (ESOP)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

New Employee Onboarding Guide

Welcome aboard! Your first 90 days.

Before Your First Day
HR will send you a welcome pack 5 business days before your start date. It includes:

 • Employment contract and GDPR consent form (please sign and return)
 • IT equipment delivery confirmation
 • Access credentials for HR portal and Slack
 • Your first-week schedule

Day 1 Checklist

Time Activity Owner

09:00 Welcome meeting with HR HR

10:00 IT setup & security training IT

11:00 Meet your manager and team Manager

12:30 Team lunch Team

14:00 Company values & culture session HR

15:30 Benefits enrolment walkthrough HR

16:30 Wrap-up & Q&A Manager

30 / 60 / 90 Day Goals

First 30 Days — Learn
 • Complete all mandatory training (GDPR, Security, Anti-bribery)
 • Shadow at least 3 different team members
 • Set up 1:1s with key stakeholders
 • Understand your team's OKRs for the current quarter

Days 31–60 — Contribute
 • Take ownership of at least one small project or task
 • Present a 'learnings so far' to your manager
 • Join at least one cross-functional meeting

Days 61–90 — Deliver
 • Complete your first formal goal-setting with manager
 • Deliver your first independent piece of work
 • Complete the 90-day survey (sent automatically by HR)

 1. Welcome
 Welcome to our organisation. This handbook outlines the policies, procedures and benefits that
 govern your employment. It is your primary reference for understanding what we expect of you and
 what you can expect from us.

 2. Working Hours & Flexible Work
 Standard working hours are 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Core hours are 10:00–16:00.
 Outside core hours employees may flex their schedule with manager approval.

 Remote Work Policy
 Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week after completing their probation period (90
 days). Full-remote arrangements require VP approval and are reviewed annually.

 • Home office allowance: €500 one-time setup + €30/month
 • Reliable internet connection required (min. 25 Mbps)
 • Must be reachable during core hours

 3. Leave Policy

Leave Type Entitlement Notice Required

Annual Leave 25 days/year 2 weeks minimum

Sick Leave Unlimited (certified) Same day (before 10:00)

Parental Leave 16 weeks primary / 6 weeks secondary8 weeks minimum

Bereavement 5 days (immediate family) As soon as possible

Study / Exam Leave 5 days/year 4 weeks minimum

Volunteering 2 days/year 2 weeks minimum

 4. Code of Conduct
 All employees are expected to act with integrity, respect, and professionalism. The following
 behaviours are strictly prohibited:

 • Harassment or bullying of any kind
 • Discrimination based on gender, age, race, religion, disability or sexual orientation
 • Misuse of company resources or confidential information
 • Conflicts of interest not disclosed to HR

[PÁGINA 2]
Violations are investigated under the Disciplinary Policy and may result in termination. Anonymous
reports can be submitted via the Ethics Hotline.

5. Benefits

Health & Wellness
 • Private health insurance (employee + 1 dependent included)
 • Mental health support: 8 free therapy sessions per year via Oliva Health
 • Gym allowance: €50/month reimbursed

Financial
 • Pension contribution: 5% employer match
 • Annual performance bonus: 5–20% of base salary
 • Employee stock options (ESOP): Level Senior and above

Learning & Development
 • €1,500/year L&D; budget per employee
 • Access to Linked In Learning, O'Reilly and Coursera
 • Internal mentoring programme — apply via HR portal

 Questions? Contact the HR team at hr@hrkb.internal | HR Portal: intranet/hr

SOURCE: 05_recruitment_and_hiring_policy.pdf | pág. 2
TITLE: Condiciones para Ofertas de Empleo y Verificación

[PÁGINA 2]
• Religious beliefs or political views
 • Disability or health conditions (pre-offer)

4. Making an Offer
Verbal offers can be made by the hiring manager after recruiter confirmation. Written offers are
prepared by HR and must include:

 • Role title, level, and department
 • Base salary and bonus target
 • Start date and probation period
 • Equity grant (if applicable)
Offers must remain within the approved salary band. Exceptions require VP and HR Director
sign-off.

5. Referral Programme
Employees can refer candidates via the HR portal. If the referred candidate is hired and completes 3
months, the referring employee receives a €1,000 cash bonus in the next payroll. Referrals cannot
be made for roles within the same reporting line.

6. Background Checks
All offers are conditional on satisfactory background checks:

 • Identity verification (passport / ID)
 • Right to work in Spain (or relevant jurisdiction)
 • Employment history verification (last 3 employers)
 • Criminal record check (roles with access to financial data or vulnerable populations)
Checks are completed by our partner (Sterling) within 5 business days.

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

| 4.5 – 5.0 | Outstanding | Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership. |
-----------------------------

Performance Review Guide

Annual Cycle 2025 | Confidential — Internal Use Only

Overview
Performance reviews at our organisation follow a bi-annual cycle: a mid-year check-in (June) and a
formal annual review (December). This guide explains the process, scoring criteria, and calibration
steps.

Review Timeline

Phase Dates Owner

Self-assessment opens 1–15 November Employee

Manager review 16–30 November Manager

 Calibration sessions 1–10 December VP + HR

Results communicated 15–20 December Manager

Compensation adjustments 1 January HR / Finance

Scoring Framework
Each employee is rated on a 1.0 – 5.0 scale across four dimensions:

Score Range Rating Label Description

1.0 – 2.0 Needs Improvement Performance below expectations. PIP required.

2.0 – 3.5 Meets Expectations Solid performer delivering on core objectives.

3.5 – 4.5 Exceeds Expectations Consistently goes above and beyond.

4.5 – 5.0 Outstanding Top 10%. Exceptional impact and leadership.

The Four Dimensions

1. Delivery & Results (35%)
Did the employee meet their OKRs? Measured against targets set at the start of the review period.
Quantitative goals carry more weight.

2. Collaboration & Teamwork (25%)
360 feedback from peers and cross-functional partners. Looks at communication, conflict resolution,
and contribution to team success.

3. Growth & Learning (20%)

[PÁGINA 2]
Evidence of skill development, certifications, internal courses completed, and application of new
knowledge to day-to-day work.

4. Leadership & Initiative (20%)
For ICs: ownership and proactivity. For managers: team development, hiring quality, and retention
of high performers.

Calibration Process
To ensure fairness across departments, all scores are calibrated before being communicated to
employees. Calibration rules:

 • Max 15% of employees can receive 'Outstanding' per department
 • Any 'Needs Improvement' rating requires documented evidence
 • Score changes during calibration must be communicated to the manager

Promotion Criteria
Promotions are considered during the annual review. Requirements:

 • Minimum 12 months in current role
 • Score of 4.0 or above in the last review
 • Manager and VP endorsement
 • Role available at the next level (for IC tracks)

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Recruitment & Hiring Policy

Version 2.4 | Applies to all hiring managers and recruiters

1. Opening a Role
To open a new or backfill position, the hiring manager must submit a Job Requisition (JR) via the
HR portal. The JR requires:

 • Approved headcount in current budget (Finance sign-off required for new headcount)
 • Job description approved by HR
 • Target start date and budget range
 • VP approval for Manager-level roles and above
Typical time from JR approval to first candidate presented: 5–10 business days.

2. Interview Process

Stage Format Owner SLA

CV screening Async review Recruiter 2 days

Recruiter call 30 min video Recruiter Day 3–5

 Hiring mgr screen 45 min video Hiring Manager Day 5–8

Technical / task Take-home or live coding Team Day 8–12

 Final panel 2x45 min (values + depth) Senior + HR Day 12–16

Offer Verbal then written HR Day 16–18

3. Interview Best Practices

Structured Interviews
All interviews must use pre-agreed structured questions from the HR question bank. Interviewers
must complete the scorecard in Lever within 24 hours.

Diversity & Inclusion
 • Panels must include at least one interviewer of a different gender than the hiring manager
 • Job descriptions reviewed by HR for inclusive language before posting
 • Blind CV screening available on request

Prohibited Questions
The following topics must never be raised in interviews:

 • Age, date of birth, or graduation year (unless relevant to eligibility)
 • Marital status or family plans
 • Nationality or country of origin

TITLE: Estructura Salarial y Revisión Anual

Compensation & Benefits Policy

Effective January 2025 | Confidential

Salary Bands
Salaries are structured around 5 levels applicable across all departments. Bands are reviewed
annually against market benchmarks (Radford / Mercer surveys). We target the 65th percentile of
the market for base salary.

Level Role Examples Band (EUR / year)

Junior Associate, Analyst I 28,000 – 38,000

Mid Specialist, Analyst II 38,000 – 55,000

Senior Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst 55,000 – 80,000

Lead Tech Lead, Team Lead 75,000 – 100,000

Manager Manager, Head of 85,000 – 120,000

Annual Pay Review
Pay reviews take place in January each year, effective 1 February. Increases are based on:
individual performance score, market movement, and position within band.

Performance Score Typical Increase

Outstanding (4.5–5.0) 6 – 10%

Exceeds Expectations (3.5–4.5) 3 – 6%

Meets Expectations (2.0–3.5) 1 – 3%

New Employee Onboarding Guide

Welcome aboard! Your first 90 days.

Before Your First Day
HR will send you a welcome pack 5 business days before your start date. It includes:

 • Employment contract and GDPR consent form (please sign and return)
 • IT equipment delivery confirmation
 • Access credentials for HR portal and Slack
 • Your first-week schedule

Day 1 Checklist

Time Activity Owner

09:00 Welcome meeting with HR HR

10:00 IT setup & security training IT

11:00 Meet your manager and team Manager

12:30 Team lunch Team

14:00 Company values & culture session HR

15:30 Benefits enrolment walkthrough HR

16:30 Wrap-up & Q&A Manager

30 / 60 / 90 Day Goals

First 30 Days — Learn
 • Complete all mandatory training (GDPR, Security, Anti-bribery)
 • Shadow at least 3 different team members
 • Set up 1:1s with key stakeholders
 • Understand your team's OKRs for the current quarter

Days 31–60 — Contribute
 • Take ownership of at least one small project or task
 • Present a 'learnings so far' to your manager
 • Join at least one cross-functional meeting

Days 61–90 — Deliver
 • Complete your first formal goal-setting with manager
 • Deliver your first independent piece of work
 • Complete the 90-day survey (sent automatically by HR)
